judgment.summary: The business combines strong revenue growth and positive free cash flow with significant negative margins and a high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS beat estimates by 17.8% (0.32 vs 0.27), with the next earnings date scheduled for 2026-05-27.
positives: Revenue growth 30.1% — shows strong top-line momentum.
Free cash flow (TTM) $1.59B — indicates cash generation despite net losses.
Analyst growth estimate (current FY) 43.5% — suggests robust future expansion.
Total cash $4.03B — provides a significant liquidity cushion.
Mean analyst target $229.66 — implies substantial upside potential.
negatives: Forward P/E 64.97 and PEG 4.22 — indicate a high valuation premium.
Profit margin -28.4% and operating margin -33.2% — reflect significant unprofitability.
Return on equity -53.9% — shows substantial losses relative to shareholder equity.
Debt/Equity 142.46 — indicates a relatively high level of leverage.

news.paragraph: Snowflake is facing recent downward pressure as its stock has declined even while the broader market has gained. Although RBC lowered its price target for the company from $245 to $220, the firm maintained an Outperform rating. There are no imminent earnings or regulatory catalysts mentioned in the current headlines.
